candidates.title: Sound-Enhanced Real Estate Reels
candidates.tools: After Effects · Premiere Pro · Animate — for video/motion editing
candidates.brief: I create educational real-estate videos—short vertical reels for Instagram and, on occasion, longer horizontal explainers. The raw material I hand over is a mix of spoken narration and property-tour visuals, but much of it is static and needs energy. I’m looking for an editor who can turn those still shots into engaging pieces through smart cuts, subtle push-ins, animated captions, tasteful transitions and, above all, professional-sounding audio work. Your job is to layer in sound effects or music that feels polished and formal, balance my voice so it sits comfortably above the track, and tighten the overall pacing so each clip holds attention from the first second to the last frame. Color correction, light grading and brand-consistent lower thirds are welcome additions, provided they never look over-produced. Typical workflow • I supply footage, a rough script outline and any reference

candidates.title: Relaxing Insomnia Video Production
candidates.tools: After Effects · Premiere Pro — for video/motion editing
candidates.brief: I have a finished script for a 10-minute YouTube video that explains how to ease insomnia. I now need the full production treatment so the final piece feels soothing, polished, and viewer-friendly. Here is the creative direction: • Overall mood: relaxing and calm, so pacing, transitions, and color grading should reinforce a sense of ease. • Visuals: lean on high-quality nature scenes—think slow-moving forests, gentle ocean waves, night skies—chosen to match each talking point in the script. Simple motion graphics or on-screen text are fine when it helps clarity, but they should never overpower the tranquil feel. • Audio: layer soft background music underneath my narration voice-over (I will supply the raw voice track, or you can record it if you have a suitable, warm tone and a treated room). Smooth fades and careful EQ are essential so music never competes with the voice. Any light ambi

candidates.title: Engaging Nepali Bag Reel Edit
candidates.tools: After Effects · Premiere Pro · InDesign — for video/motion editing
candidates.brief: I run a bag store and already have several raw clips of my products in action. I now need those shots turned into a single, highly engaging reel that will sit perfectly on social channels. Here is what matters most to me: • The reel’s core purpose is a clean, punchy product showcase. • Voiceover must be in Nepali, delivered with an energetic vibe that instantly hooks viewers. • Layer upbeat background music beneath the narration so the final mix feels lively but never overpowers the voice. • Smooth, modern edits—think quick cuts, light motion graphics or text where it helps, and color tweaks that make the bags pop. Deliverables 1. One vertical reel (1080×1920) in MP4, optimized for Instagram & Facebook. 2. Separate audio master (voice + music) in WAV or high-quality MP3. 3. A short caption suggestion that matches the reel’s energy (optional but appreciated). I’ll share the raw clips via

candidates.title: Step-By-Step Tutorial Video Editing -- 2
candidates.tools: After Effects · Premiere Pro — for scalable vector artwork and video/motion editing
candidates.brief: I have recorded a full tutorial and now need a skilled editor to transform the raw footage into a clear, engaging, step-by-step lesson. The final video should flow logically from one step to the next, keeping viewers focused on the process while feeling visually supported throughout. Here is what I’m after: • Clean, polished pacing that eliminates mistakes, long pauses, and off-topic moments. • Smooth transitions between steps so the learning curve feels natural. • Strategic on-screen callouts—whether that ends up being text overlays, simple animations, or helpful graphics/diagrams—placed only where they truly add clarity. • Balanced audio levels and, if needed, subtle background music that never distracts from my voiceover. • Exported in HD (1080p or higher) with a high-quality codec ready for upload. candidates.title: Nepali Horror Recap Editor
candidates.brief: I run a small YouTube channel that breaks down classic and modern horror films for Nepali viewers, and I’m ready to hand the editing reins to someone local who truly understands the genre’s tension and timing. Here’s what each video needs to feel complete: • 10–15 minutes of tight, suspense-driven storytelling cut from my provided voice-over and raw clips • Seamless transitions, jump cuts, zoom-ins, and text callouts that keep the pace brisk without losing clarity • Eerie yet balanced background music, stingers, and spot sound-effects that heighten scares while never overpowering narration • Colour and audio levels matched across sources so the final file looks professional on YouTube in 1080p I’m still a student, so the fixed rate is 100 INR per finished video; in return, you’ll receive consistent weekly work and plenty of creative freedom as we tackle everything from black-and-white cu

candidates.title: Documentary Video Editor — Maritime YouTube Channel
candidates.brief: Fathomy is a faceless YouTube documentary channel covering maritime mysteries, ship disappearances, and historical events at sea. Our videos are 14–18 minutes long, cinematic and narration-driven — dark, atmospheric documentary storytelling. WHAT YOU WILL DO: - Edit documentary videos from a fully recorded voiceover audio file - Source B-roll footage and archival images to match the narration - Select and layer background music under the voiceover - Apply a consistent dark cinematic color grade across all videos - Add text overlays, lower thirds, and simple motion graphics - Deliver finished exports ready for YouTube within 3–5 business days REQUIREMENTS: - Proficient in DaVinci Resolve or Adobe Premiere Pro - Strong color grading skills — dark and cinematic look - Experience editing documentary or narrative content specifically - Able to source royalty-free footage independently - Relia
